papers.title_en: Stochastic Thermodynamics and SDE-based Generative Models
papers.evidence: 为薛定谔桥和扩散模型提供了热力学框架
papers.tldr: SDE基生成模型（如扩散模型、薛定谔桥）在语音增强、图像修复等任务中广泛应用，但其与热力学的关系尚不明确。本文在随机热力学框架下为这类模型建立了轨迹层面的功、热和熵产生定义，并推导出广义Jarzynski恒等式和类第二定律不等式。该方法将原始Jarzynski设置扩展至时变浴温和非保守驱动力，为理解扩散模型与薛定谔桥提供了非平衡统计力学视角。
papers.abstract_en: SDE-based generative models, including diffusion models and the Schrödinger bridge, have found broad applications in signal processing tasks such as speech enhancement, image restoration, and time-series generation. This note presents a modeling framework for such models within the context of stochastic thermodynamics. The main results of this note are trajectory-level definitions of work, heat, and entropy production, along with a generalized Jarzynski identity and a second-law-like inequality. The proposed framework extends the original Jarzynski setup to accommodate time-dependent bath temperature and nonconservative driving forces. This thermodynamic perspective may deepen our understanding of diffusion models and the Schrödinger bridge from a nonequilibrium statistical mechanics viewpoint.

papers.title_en: Variational Test-time Optimization for Diffusion Synchronization
papers.authors: Hyunsoo Lee, Farrin Marouf Sofian, Kushagra Pandey, Stephan Mandt
papers.evidence: 通过最优控制协调多个扩散轨迹，类似于桥接过程
papers.tldr: 协同生成利用多个扩散轨迹扩展预训练模型能力，但现有扩散同步方法依赖启发式且需任务特定调整。本文从最优控制理论推导同步框架，在采样时优化控制变量，使多轨迹保持与扩散先验接近的同时达成协调。测试时优化无需训练，在多个模态的协同生成任务上超越基线。该方法为协同生成建立了原则性基础，可广泛适配不同场景。
papers.abstract_en: Collaborative generation, which coordinates multiple diffusion trajectories to extend the capabilities of pretrained priors, has emerged as a powerful paradigm for extending the applicability of diffusion models. Among existing approaches, diffusion synchronization provides a scenario-agnostic solution by introducing general guidance mechanisms. However, current synchronization approaches rely heavily on heuristics and still require task-specific tailoring, which limits their generalizability and performance. In this work, we mathematically derive a synchronization framework based on optimal control, providing a principled explanation of diffusion synchronization. During sampling, we optimize control variables to guide multiple trajectories toward coherent solutions while remaining close to the underlying diffusion prior. Our method operates entirely at test-time without additional training, thereby enabling broad applicability across diverse generation scenarios when combined with strong pretrained priors. We demonstrate consistent improvements over baselines on three representative collaborative generation tasks, covering a wide range of modalities and applications. Beyond performance gains, our work establishes a novel foundation for collaborative generation, opening a principled path toward extending pretrained generative models to new collaborative generation settings.

papers.title_en: Diffusion Flow Matching: Dimension-Improved KL Bounds and Wasserstein Guarantees
papers.authors: Marta Gentiloni Silveri, Giovanni Conforti, Alain Durmus
papers.evidence: 扩散流匹配的理论保证，与构造桥相关
papers.tldr: 扩散流匹配（DFM）是一种新兴生成模型框架，但其收敛理论尚不完全。本文针对基于布朗运动的DFM，在KL散度下给出改进的维数依赖界，达到当前最优；在2-Wasserstein距离下也获得类似维数依赖的收敛保证。该成果在最小假设条件下提供了精细的离散化误差分析，推动DFM理论发展。
papers.abstract_en: Diffusion Flow Matching (DFM) has recently emerged as a versatile framework for generative modeling, yet its theoretical convergence properties remain only partially understood. In this work, we provide refined and novel convergence guarantees for Brownian motion based DFMs, focusing on the discretization error. Our analysis is conducted under the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence and the 2-Wasserstein distance. Under finite-moment conditions and a mild score integrability assumption, we derive KL convergence bounds with improved dimensional dependence compared to prior work, achieving, up to our knowledge, state-of-the-art scaling under minimal conditions. We further extend the analysis to the 2-Wasserstein distance: under an additional first-order score integrability assumption and a weak log-concavity condition, we obtain convergence guarantees with dimensional dependence consistent with the KL case.
